what_it_does: This file is a JSON allocation batch that lists six source files from the claude-ds project, identifying each by project name, relative path, and absolute root directory.
observations: All six entries share the same project value claude-ds and root /home/tim/company/design/claude-ds.
The list contains two paired TypeScript declaration and JSX implementation files: Tabs and ContainmentTree.
It also includes core/archetype-catalog.js and core/containers.css alongside the component files.
There is no symbols array or additional metadata inside the content.
The file path encodes a wave, allocation directory, and batch number: wave-002/alloc/batch-025.json.
standouts: Paired .d.ts and .jsx entries for Tabs and ContainmentTree.
Inclusion of both a JS archetype catalog and a CSS containers file in the same batch as components.
conventions: Each record follows a uniform {project, path, root} schema.
The root path is an absolute filesystem path.
Batch files are organized under wave-N/alloc/batch-M.json.
concerns: No stated rationale for why these specific files are grouped together.
No version, timestamp, or batch metadata beyond the filename.
Absolute root path is tied to a specific local environment.
No symbols field is present even though the ledger is meant to record code/design substrates.
notes: A batch manifest that designates six claude-ds source files for an interpretive or fusion step.
questions: What downstream process consumes this allocation batch?
Why were these six files selected for batch 025 in wave 002?
Are the .d.ts/.jsx pairs intended to be processed as declaration-plus-implementation sets?
What does wave-002 signify in the broader workflow?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename and directory suggest it is an allocation batch, and the content is exactly a list of allocated source files, so purpose and content align.
apparent_themes: grouping core support files with components
intention_signals: It appears to designate a focused, coherent slice of the claude-ds design system for downstream interpretation, fusion, or analysis.
contribution: It provides a scoped, machine-readable manifest of source files that a fused system can use as an input boundary for processing.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON allocation batch file listing six claude-ds design-system source files, including paired component declarations and implementations plus core catalog and CSS files.
